representative.title: [correctness] Ledger evidence still records only summary counts
representative.rationale: The new build ledger claims to dogfood harness-captured evidence, but both task evidence blocks contain only synthesized summary counts (`# tests 149`, `# pass 149`, `# fail 0`) instead of the actual per-test `ok N - <name>` output. That is the summary-only degradation AC-1/AC-2 are meant to close, so this PR's own recorded evidence still contradicts the stated discipline.
representative.suggestion: Replace the summary-only test lines for each task with the actual conductor-run `node --test` output, or a bounded tail/cap that still includes the per-test names the proof map may cite.

report:
# Review (2 clusters)
- 🟠 **[MEDIUM]** \[correctness\] Ledger evidence still records only summary counts — `specs/harness-captured-evidence/build-report.md:36` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(correctness)_
  The new build ledger claims to dogfood harness-captured evidence, but both task evidence blocks contain only synthesized summary counts (\`\# tests 149\`, \`\# pass 149\`, \`\# fail 0\`) instead of the actual per-test \`ok N - \<name\>\` output. That is the summary-only degradation AC-1/AC-2 are meant to close, so this PR's own recorded evidence still contradicts the stated discipline.
  _Fix:_ Replace the summary-only test lines for each task with the actual conductor-run \`node --test\` output, or a bounded tail/cap that still includes the per-test names the proof map may cite.
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[correctness\] Tail-cap criterion references a proof map that doesn't exist yet at per-task capture time — `agent-sdlc/skills/build/SKILL.md:94` · 1/2 models · _by:_ holistic _(correctness)_
  The bounded-suite guidance says to cap the block to a tail 'but retain the per-test names of any test the proof map will cite' — yet the proof map is authored later at ship, so at per-task capture the conductor cannot know which names will be cited, leaving the cap criterion under-determined. The safe reading (retain all per-test names) works and the build-complete full-listing fallback covers it, so AC-14 breakage risk is low, but the forward reference is ambiguous.
  _Fix:_ Reword to a builder-evaluable rule, e.g. 'cap only the non-test noise; retain every per-test \`ok N - \<name\>\` line in full' — since the builder can't yet know the proof map's citations, the safe invariant is keep all per-test names.
prComment:
## Review Gate — Round 1
🚫 **BLOCK** — 1 blocking finding(s) must be resolved or justified.

Findings: 2 total — 0 critical · 0 high · 1 medium · 1 low · 0 info.
_Reviewed by:_ holistic + lens-spec · models: codex:gpt-5.5, claude:claude-opus-4-8

### Must fix
- 🟠 **[MEDIUM]** \[correctness\] Ledger evidence still records only summary counts — `specs/harness-captured-evidence/build-report.md:36` · 1/2 models · _by:_ lens-spec _(correctness)_
  The new build ledger claims to dogfood harness-captured evidence, but both task evidence blocks contain only synthesized summary counts (\`\# tests 149\`, \`\# pass 149\`, \`\# fail 0\`) instead of the actual per-test \`ok N - \<name\>\` output. That is the summary-only degradation AC-1/AC-2 are meant to close, so this PR's own recorded evidence still contradicts the stated discipline.
  _Fix:_ Replace the summary-only test lines for each task with the actual conductor-run \`node --test\` output, or a bounded tail/cap that still includes the per-test names the proof map may cite.

### Advisory (non-blocking)
- ⚪ **[LOW]** \[correctness\] Tail-cap criterion references a proof map that doesn't exist yet at per-task capture time — `agent-sdlc/skills/build/SKILL.md:94` · 1/2 models · _by:_ holistic _(correctness)_
  The bounded-suite guidance says to cap the block to a tail 'but retain the per-test names of any test the proof map will cite' — yet the proof map is authored later at ship, so at per-task capture the conductor cannot know which names will be cited, leaving the cap criterion under-determined. The safe reading (retain all per-test names) works and the build-complete full-listing fallback covers it, so AC-14 breakage risk is low, but the forward reference is ambiguous.
  _Fix:_ Reword to a builder-evaluable rule, e.g. 'cap only the non-test noise; retain every per-test \`ok N - \<name\>\` line in full' — since the builder can't yet know the proof map's citations, the safe invariant is keep all per-test names.
